reasoning: The Lean statement is exactly the standard Mathlib lemma `EReal.limsup_add_le`. The informal "provided the sum on the right is not of the form ∞−∞" is faithfully encoded by the two disjunctive hypotheses: `h1` rules out the case (limsup a = −∞ ∧ limsup b = +∞) and `h2` rules out (limsup a = +∞ ∧ limsup b = −∞). Together these are exactly the cases where the EReal sum would be "∞−∞". The sequences are real (ℕ → ℝ), coerced to EReal so the limsup lives in EReal, matching the informal notion where the limsup can be ±∞. The conclusion `limsup (a+b) ≤ limsup a + limsup b` matches precisely. No hypothesis is dropped or added beyond what the informal statement specifies.
